perturbation.original_text: TERM: The rights granted hereunder shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2006 (the "Term"); provided, however, that with respect to each Property, including Robots, Kingdom of Heaven, Mr. and Mrs. Smith, In Her Shoes, Idiocracy (Oww My Balls) and Ice Age II, all right and licenses granted herein will continue in full force and effect until March 31, 2008.
perturbation.changed_text: TERM: The rights granted hereunder shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2006 (the "Term"); provided, however, that with respect to each Property, including Robots, Kingdom of Heaven, Mr. and Mrs. Smith, In Her Shoes, Idiocracy (Oww My Balls) and Ice Age II, all right and licenses granted herein will continue in full force and effect until March 31, 2008, unless otherwise terminated earlier by Fox at its sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the rights for specific properties will continue until March 31, 2008. The modified text adds "unless otherwise terminated earlier by Fox at its sole discretion." This introduces a contradiction. The original implied a fixed term until March 31, 2008, while the modified version gives Fox the unilateral right to terminate those rights earlier, creating uncertainty about the actual duration of the license. Therefore the duration of licenses, as defined in this part of the contract becomes uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXTENSION OF TERM: The first paragraph of Section 4 of the Agreement is hereby deleted in its entirety and replaced with the following: "TERM: The rights granted hereunder shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2006 (the "Term"); provided, however, that with respect to each Property, including Robots, Kingdom of Heaven, Mr. and Mrs. Smith, In Her Shoes, Idiocracy (Oww My Balls) and Ice Age II, all right and licenses granted herein will continue in full force and effect until March 31, 2008, unless otherwise terminated earlier by Fox at its sole discretion."
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a clause allowing Fox to terminate the rights earlier than March 31, 2008, which directly contradicts the original text's implication of a fixed term. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, reinforcing the presence of the newly introduced contradiction within the document itself. This unilateral termination right creates uncertainty and directly impacts the enforceability of the rights granted until March 31, 2008, causing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Except as herein expressly amended or by necessary implication modified by this Amendment, the Agreement in all other respects is hereby ratified and shall continue in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as herein expressly amended or by necessary implication modified by this Amendment, the Agreement in all other respects is hereby ratified and shall continue in full force and effect, provided that Fox may unilaterally modify any other section of the original agreement with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original text ratifies the original agreement, stating it remains in full force. The modified version adds that Fox can unilaterally modify *any* other section with 30 days' notice. This creates a contradiction. Ratification implies the original agreement remains unchanged except for explicitly stated amendments. Allowing unilateral modification undermines this ratification, because it becomes uncertain on how the contract is in full force if Fox, the administrator, has the unilateral right to modify the terms. In fact, because of the ratification being a broad clause, it contradicts the modification being a broad clause as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as herein expressly amended or by necessary implication modified by this Amendment, the Agreement in all other respects is hereby ratified and shall continue in full force and effect, provided that Fox may unilaterally modify any other section of the original agreement with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The original agreement is ratified to be 'in full force and effect'. The changed text adds a clause that allows Fox to unilaterally modify any section of the agreement with 30 days' notice. This creates a direct contradiction, as 'full force and effect' implies the agreement remains as is, barring explicit amendments. The unilateral modification clause allows for changes beyond the explicitly stated amendments, thereby contradicting the initial ratification. The contradiction is further emphasized because the Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, demonstrating that the change itself introduces the conflicting elements. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction', specifically when a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.
